title: DeepSeek Founded
description: Founded by Liang Wenfeng, founder of High-Flyer Quant, in Hangzhou. DeepSeek focuses on Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) research. The company spun off from High-Flyer Quant's AI research department, initially equipped with tens of thousands of A100 GPUs.

title: DeepSeek Coder First Released
description: Launched the first open-source code large model DeepSeek Coder, supporting multiple programming languages with outstanding performance in code generation and completion tasks. Marks the beginning of DeepSeek's open-source strategy.

title: DeepSeek-LLM 67B Released
description: Released the DeepSeek-LLM series, including 7B and 67B parameter versions, fully open-sourced on GitHub. The models performed close to top-tier levels in both English and Chinese benchmark tests.

title: DeepSeek-MoE Released
description: Launched the DeepSeek-MoE model based on Mixture-of-Experts architecture, achieving efficient inference with approximately 16B activated parameters out of 145B total parameters, significantly reducing training and inference costs.

title: DeepSeek-V2 Released
description: Released DeepSeek-V2 with 236B total parameters but only 21B activated per inference. Its innovative MLA (Multi-head Latent Attention) architecture reduced inference costs to 1/70 of GPT-4 Turbo, triggering a global AI 'price war'.

title: DeepSeek-V2 Open-Sourced and Triggers Price War
description: DeepSeek-V2 entered the market at extremely low API pricing (only 1 RMB per million tokens), forcing ByteDance, Alibaba, Baidu, and others to cut prices. Dubbed by the industry as 'the catalyst of the AI price war'.

title: DeepSeek-Coder-V2 Released
description: Released DeepSeek-Coder-V2, surpassing GPT-4 Turbo in code generation tasks, supporting 338 programming languages and 128K context length. Also adopts MoE architecture and is fully open-sourced.

title: DeepSeek Math Model DeepSeek-Math-7B Gains Attention
description: Research papers show DeepSeek-Math-7B approaches GPT-4 level in mathematics competition-level benchmarks, proving that small models trained on high-quality data can also achieve astonishing performance.

title: DeepSeek-V3 Released
description: Released DeepSeek-V3 with 671B total parameters (37B activated), training cost of only approximately $5.576 million, completed in two months using 2,048 H800 GPUs. Performance rivals GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet.

title: DeepSeek-V3 Open-Sourced and Shakes Industry
description: DeepSeek-V3 fully open-sourced model weights on GitHub. Its extremely low training cost and powerful performance sparked global AI community discussion, regarded as an important milestone challenging Western AI giants.

title: DeepSeek-R1 Released
description: Released reasoning model DeepSeek-R1, matching OpenAI o1 in mathematics, code, and logical reasoning tasks. Adopted a fully open-source strategy releasing technical reports and model weights. Triggered global tech stock volatility.

title: DeepSeek-R1 Tops US App Store
description: DeepSeek app surpassed ChatGPT to reach #1 on the US App Store free apps chart. On the same day, NVIDIA stock plummeted 17%, with approximately $589 billion in market cap erased, setting a record for the largest single-day market value loss in US stock market history.

title: OpenAI Alleges DeepSeek Distillation Controversy
description: OpenAI and Microsoft stated they are investigating whether DeepSeek used OpenAI model outputs for distillation training. This controversy sparked widespread discussion about the boundaries between open-source and closed-source models.

title: Multiple Government Agencies Restrict DeepSeek Usage
description: US Navy, NASA, and multiple government agencies banned or restricted DeepSeek usage citing data security concerns. Australia, South Korea, Japan, and other countries also launched security reviews of DeepSeek.

title: DeepSeek-R1 Adopted by Major Cloud Platforms
description: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Huawei Cloud, Alibaba Cloud, and other major global cloud service providers successively launched DeepSeek-R1 and DeepSeek-V3, marking the mainstream adoption of open-source models.

title: DeepSeek Announces Next-Phase Multimodal Plans
description: Liang Wenfeng revealed in an interview that DeepSeek is developing multimodal models and next-generation reasoning systems, emphasizing continued commitment to the open-source route and extreme cost optimization strategy.

title: High-Flyer Quant Announces AGI Research Investment
description: High-Flyer Quant publicly announced it would devote significant resources to Artificial General Intelligence research, revealing reserves of over 10,000 A100 GPUs, laying the foundation for the subsequent establishment of DeepSeek.

title: DeepSeek Publishes Multiple Research Papers
description: DeepSeek research team published multiple technical papers, detailing innovations such as the MLA attention mechanism and auxiliary-loss-free load balancing. These technologies became key to low-cost large model training.

title: DeepSeek-R1-Lite-Preview Released Ahead of Schedule
description: Before the official R1 release, DeepSeek launched R1-Lite-Preview to demonstrate its reasoning capabilities. The model outperformed o1-preview on mathematics benchmarks such as AIME and MATH.

title: DeepSeek Team Publishes Fire-Flyer AI-HPC Paper
description: Published a paper on the Fire-Flyer 2 AI supercomputing cluster, detailing how to build efficient AI training infrastructure at extremely low cost, demonstrating deep expertise in hardware efficiency and software optimization.

title: DeepSeek-V4 Development Rumors Emerge
description: Industry sources say DeepSeek is developing its next-generation model V4, expected to further enhance multimodal capabilities and reasoning efficiency, with a possible mid-2025 release.

title: DeepSeek Releases Second-Generation Code Model
description: Before Coder-V2, DeepSeek continued to iterate its code models, gradually expanding supported programming language types and context windows, building developer community reputation.

title: DeepSeek Open Source Week Event
description: DeepSeek held Open Source Week, releasing multiple core tool libraries and optimization solutions over five consecutive days, including FlashMLA, DeepEP, DeepGEMM, etc., further consolidating its open-source leader image.

title: DeepSeek First Chat Model Enters Internal Testing
description: DeepSeek began internal testing of its first conversational AI product, preparing for subsequent public release. The model was specifically optimized for Chinese understanding and generation.

title: DeepSeek Technical Report Reveals Training Details
description: Released V2 technical report detailing MoE architecture, data processing pipeline, and training methodology, becoming an important reference for industry research on low-cost large model training.
